title: Tender Crumbly Vietnamese Gouda Cheese Nem Nuong
description: A delicious, satvik-friendly, one-pot Vietnamese dish featuring crumbly Gouda cheese, herbs, and a broiled scallop-shell vessel.
instructions: Clean and prepare the scallop shells by rinsing them under cold water and drying them with a paper towel.
Cut the Gouda cheese into small crumbles.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the chopped herbs, garlic, ginger, salt, and black pepper.
Add the coconut milk and lime juice to the bowl and mix until well combined.
Add the crumbled Gouda cheese to the bowl and mix until fully incorporated.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the herb-coconut cheese mixture to the skillet and stir constantly until the cheese is melted and the mixture is heated through.
Spoon the mixture into the prepared scallop shells and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Broil the nem nuong in the preheated oven for 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is golden brown and the shells are lightly charred.
Serve the nem nuong hot, garnished with additional fresh herbs if desired.
